exemplar.title: Investigating whether there is a correlation between the concentration of copper sulfate (62.5 ppm, 125 ppm, 250 ppm, 500 ppm, 1000 ppm) and the germination and early growth of mung beans (Vigna radiata) as measured through the change in shoot length of germinated mung bean seeds after 5 days?

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating whether there is a correlation between the concentration of copper sulfate (62.5 ppm, 125 ppm, 250 ppm, 500 ppm, 1000 ppm) and the germination and early growth of mung beans (Vigna radiata) as measured through the change in shoot length of germinated mung bean seeds after 5 days?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

files.fileName: Biology IA final final draft newest (1).pdf

strengths: Research question is situated within a specific and appropriate agricultural context.
Background integrates copper toxicity ranges and plant micronutrient roles effectively.
Procedure is detailed enough to allow reproduction with few ambiguities.
weaknesses: Rationale for sample size and duration is underdeveloped.
Minor omissions and reagent misnaming (NaCl vs CuSOâ) affect reproducibility.
Some monitoredâvariables description is fragmented and lacks clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (methodological considerations and methodology description) are solidly in the 3â4 band, while one strand (context) reaches the lower 5â6 band. Overall performance sits in the 3â4 band, so a holistic score of 3 reflects both strengths and remaining gaps.

strengths: Data tables and graph formatting (with error bars and units) are communicated clearly.
Uncertainties are quoted and partially discussed.
Relevant statistical processing (means, SD, correlation) is attempted.
weaknesses: Instrumental uncertainties are incomplete and not propagated into final values.
Transcription errors and a mismatch in data collection duration undermine precision.
Choice of Pearsonâs correlation for a non-linear trend is inappropriate, and major omissions persist.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although communication of data is clear in places, significant omissions, inconsistencies and inaccuracies limit performance to the lower part of the 1â2 band, warranting a score of 2.

strengths: Conclusion correctly identifies the promotion/inhibition trend in relation to mean shoot lengths.
Mechanistic explanations (ROS, osmotic stress) are linked to accepted scientific context.
Comparison to literature is relevant and supports the studentâs interpretation.
weaknesses: Numerical inconsistencies between text and tables remain.
Some causal language overstates the statistical association.
Depth of critical analysis could be increased with clearer caveats.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is relevant and largely consistent with the analysis (3â4 band), with effective scientific context comparison. Minor inconsistencies and overstatements position it at the lower end of the 3â4 band.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and linked to their relative impact on results.
Improvements proposed are realistic and directly relevant to the identified limitations.
Discussion of statistical power (sample size) demonstrates critical evaluation.
weaknesses: Some improvement suggestions lack implementation detail (e.g., precise equipment/protocol).
A few explanations are repetitive rather than deepening the analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation shows clear explanation of specific limitations and relevant improvements, aligning with the 5â6 band. Minor superficial elements justify placing the criterion at the upper 3â4 / lower 5â6 range, earning a score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states and interprets a trend (promotion at low concentration, inhibition at high) and connects this explicitly to the processed means. While some numerical inconsistencies remain, the conclusion is largely consistent with the presented analysis, fulfilling the 3-4 band. Coherence warrants the upper score of 4.

Raw data tables, processed tables and a labelled graph with error bars are presented; uncertainties are quoted with units; headings are clear. Formatting glitches notwithstanding, the information is communicated in a manner that is clear, though not immaculate. Both clarity and precision are broadly achieved, justifying the top of the 3-4 band (4).

Means, standard deviations and a correlation coefficient are calculated appropriately, yet transcription errors appear (e.g., text cites 5.62 cm for 62.5 ppm that disagrees with table), and the choice of linear correlation for an evidently non-linear pattern is questionable. Overall processing is relevant but exhibits notable inaccuracies, matching the 3-4 descriptor; the lower mark of 3 reflects these issues.

The report identifies several specific methodological weaknesses (seed variability, light, water distribution, observation length) and for each explains how it could distort the dependent variable, thereby discussing relative impact. The explanations are substantive though sometimes repetitive, aligning with the 5-6 descriptor; minor superficial passages justify awarding 5 rather than 6.

Mechanistic explanations involving micronutrient roles, ROS production and osmotic stress are cited with references, demonstrating relevant comparison to accepted scientific understanding. Depth is good but not thoroughly critical, so the strand comfortably achieves the 3-4 band at 4 marks.

The student locates the research question within a clearly defined agricultural context: the accumulation of copper in cultivated soils and its potential effects on crop establishment. Background information on copper toxicity ranges in soils, plant micronutrient roles and the practical relevance of mung-bean germination is provided, showing a direct and specific contextual link. This exceeds the broad outline expected in the 3-4 band and fits the descriptor of a âspecific and appropriate contextâ. Minor stylistic errors do not materially reduce clarity, so a solid score at the lower end of the 5-6 band (5) is appropriate.

Instrument uncertainties are listed, SD error bars plotted and briefly discussed. However, measurement uncertainty is not propagated into means, and the distinction between SD and experimental uncertainty is blurred. Treatment of the Pearson critical value is correct but unrelated to instrumental error. Significant omissions lower this to the mid-band level (3).

For every cited weakness the student proposes a logical, feasible improvement (uniform seed selection, controlled lighting, drip irrigation, extended duration, larger sample). Rationale is given but not always deeply analysed, fitting the 3-4 band. The clarity and direct link to weaknesses justify the upper score of 4.

Key methodological considerationsâchoice of concentration range based on pre-tests, justification for 2-fold serial dilution, volume selection, and identification of major controlled and monitored variablesâare described and generally linked to securing relevant data. However, the rationale for sample size (only five seeds per treatment) and duration (five days) is weak and potential sufficiency issues are not fully addressed. Because explanations are partial rather than thorough, the work sits in the upper part of the 3-4 band.

The step-by-step procedure specifies masses, volumes, concentrations, numbers of seeds and time frames such that another investigator could realistically reproduce the investigation. Ambiguities remain (e.g., exact cupboard temperature, daily water loss, positioning of replicates), but these are âfewâ rather than major. This matches the descriptor for 3-4 marks, and the clarity merits the upper value of 4.

annotations.comment: The research question is situated within a specific agricultural context and clearly links copper sulfate concentration to mung bean growth, demonstrating strong contextual grounding.
Uncertainties are listed for some materials but omitted for cotton and beakers. Provide measurement uncertainties for all items to support uncertainty analysis.
Pearsonâs correlation is applied to a clearly nonlinear pattern. Consider fitting a nonlinear model or using ANOVA to assess significance more appropriately.
Interpreting correlation as causation overstates the statistical result. Reframe conclusions to describe associations rather than definitive causeâeffect links.
The research question statement cuts off mid-sentence, making its scope ambiguous. Complete the phrasing to ensure clarity on what is being measured and compared.
The dissociation equation is miswritten as âCuâ+â rather than $$Cu^{2+}$$. It should read $$\mathrm{CuSO_4\cdot5H_2O \rightarrow Cu^{2+} + SO_4^{2-} + 5H_2O}$$.
The background section provides detailed information on copper toxicity ranges, plant micronutrient roles and practical relevance, demonstrating strong integration of context.
The hypothesis is clearly stated, linking low copper concentrations to positive shoot growth and anticipating inhibition at high levels, showing logical continuity from background knowledge.
The pretest section outlines concentrations and volumes but omits details on the number of replicates and quantitative results. Include a data table of pretest outcomes.
Pre-test 1 description lacks the number of seeds per treatment and statistical summaries. Specify replicates and include mean growth data to justify dilution ranges.
Figure captions are inconsistent (e.g., âFigure 3, Weighing the set upâ). Standardize numbering and caption format (e.g., âFigure 3. Weighing the setup.â).
The rationale for using 2-fold serial dilution is noted, but it would be stronger if linked to literature or pretest data showing why this range captures growth variation.
The table of independent variables (Table 1) is formatted ambiguously with empty headers. Revise the structure to clearly align variables with their descriptions.
The controlled variables table provides detailed identification of variables, reasoning and control methods, enabling reproducibility and validity of the procedure.
The monitored variables table spans multiple text blocks and cuts off mid-sentence, making methods unclear. Consolidate the content within table cells for clarity.
The safety table systematically lists hazards, risks and controls, demonstrating thorough attention to laboratory safety requirements.
The method for preparing the stock solution incorrectly refers to sodium chloride instead of copper sulfate. Correct the reagent description to ensure reproducibility.
Raw data are reported for a 7-day period, whereas the methodology specifies 5 days. Ensure consistency between the procedure and data collection duration.
The formula for the mean is obscured by bullet placeholders. Present it clearly in LaTeX, for example $$\bar x = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^n x_i}{n}$$ to enhance precision.
Outlier determination is described correctly, but the analysis does not propagate instrumental uncertainties into the means and standard deviations. Include this step for completeness.
The mean value for 1000âppm is reported as 5.62âcm in text yet appears as 5.6âcm in the table. Verify calculations and ensure consistency throughout.
The evaluation of sample size as a weakness with linked improvement toEnable t-tests shows insight into statistical power and demonstrates effective evaluation.
The header â14â in the limitations table is unclear and appears misplaced. Replace it with a descriptive column title to improve readability.
The discussion integrates reactive oxygen species and osmotic stress mechanisms well, linking biochemical context to observed trends and reinforcing scientific consistency.
Limitations such as seed variability and its impact on results are described with clear rationale, demonstrating strong critical evaluation of methodological weaknesses.
Improvement suggestions are realistic but lack specific implementation details (e.g., equipment model or protocol), reducing clarity on how to execute them.
One reference (GraphPad FAQ) lacks full citation details such as publication date and publisher. Include complete information for proper attribution.
A redundant table appears at the top of page 17 (Table 10), duplicating weaknesses and improvements. Remove or merge it to avoid confusion.
The references list contains inconsistent formatting across entries. Apply a consistent referencing style (e.g., APA) to improve professionalism and accuracy.
